observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct:

a.	the GSO-Designate to prepare and submit the IIRUP to the Disposal Committee;

b.	the Disposal Committee to undertake the disposal process of the unserviceable properties as provided in COA-DBM JC No. 2024-01 to realize possible income source for the local government; and

c.	the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to record any impairment loss as required under IPSAS No. 21 to present fairly the affected accounts in the financial statements.

To properly classify the affected accounts, we recommended the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary correcting entries to close the affected PPE and Accumulated Depreciation accounts to the Prior Period Adjustment account. Further, we recommended the GSO-Designate to replace the previously issued Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) or Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR) with Inventory Custodian Slips (ICS) as required under Section 3.7.2 of COA Circular No. 2024-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

the necessary adjusting journal entries to reverse the long outstanding stale checks, correcting the understatement in the Cash in Bank – LCCA account; and
Ensure timely preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements for all Cash in Bank – LCSA accounts to ensure accuracy and reliability of the reported balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to avail of the guidelines and apply the procedures on the one-time cleansing of dormant receivables provided under COA Circular No. 2023-008 for the fair presentation of the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require:

the Municipal Accountant to prepare a written inquiry with the SAs if they have in their records receivables from the LGU amounting to the stated balances and return the same, otherwise, prepare a journal entry voucher to transfer such amount to the GF and treat the same as income; and
the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to return the balance of ₱3,853.87 to the SA in conformity with Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to review and correct the CIP – Infrastructure schedule to ensure project balances are accurately and individually presented in accordance with supporting documents. Furthermore, regular reconciliation between the CIP schedule and journals should be standardized to ensure accuracy, completeness and reliability of reported balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to enforce stricter procedures to ensure that Sales or Commercial Invoices are secured before processing payments to suppliers. This will strengthen compliance with documentary requirements and uphold the integrity of the procurement process.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to only provide the maximum appropriations for the payment of Subsistence Allowance considering the limitations provided by Item 8.3 of DBM-DOH JC No. 1, S. 2012 and Item 6.2 of DBM-DSWD JC No. 1, S. 2018;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to use the appropriate account titles in the recognition of laundry allowances and hazard pay to ensure the accuracy of reported balances of affected accounts; and
Instruct the HRMO to prepare the payroll for the grant of Subsistence Allowance in the month succeeding the actual services rendered to ascertain the accuracy and validity of said claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant revert back the unexpended balance of Trust Liabilities for CY 2018 from Trust Fund to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 to make the fund available for other social services.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:

instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to immediately start the procurement process of all the programs/projects funded out of the RA No. 7171 funds which were not yet subjected to public bidding, and
require all concerned Department Heads to facilitate the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to give the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds the early enjoyment of benefits they rightfully deserve. Thus, immediate implementation would enable the municipal government to avail of lesser project/program costs considering the escalating prices of all commodities, hence more benefits could be derived from these funds.

We recommended that Management implement the PPAs as planned in order to maximize the utilization of the fund as intended and ultimately achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes in the municipality as provided under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular Nos. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017 and 2020-01 dated March 27, 2020 and DOF-DILG-DBM JMC No. 2020-01 dated November 4, 2020.

We recommended that Management:
Oblige the BAC and Municipal Engineer to review all contracts pertaining to the related infrastructure projects to implement calibrated actions provided in GPPB Circular No. 03-2019 prior to termination in part or in whole, of the work that has not been completed and accepted under a contract.
Impose administrative sanctions, if warranted, in accordance with Section 69 of the IRR of RA No.  9184, now aligned with Section 100 (k) of the RA No. 12009, the New Government Procurement Act. to protect the interest of the government and to prevent similar incurrence of extended delays in the implementation of the projects; and
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to conduct necessary field surveys and investigations in the detailed engineering and carefully study/evaluate the site condition in the preparation of accurate design plans, technical specifications, quantity and cost estimates and program of work for infrastructure projects to minimize any work suspension that may cause delay in the completion of the projects, thus the interest of the government would not be compromised and the benefits would be delivered timely to its constituents.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to strictly monitor the implementation of infrastructure projects through validation of the contractor’s performance and by implementing calibrated measures if delays are incurred so as not to defer the immediate benefits that the constituents could derive therefrom.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC through its Secretariat to post the NOA in the PhilGEPS within 3 calendar days from the date of its issuance, consistent with the timelines previously set under Section 37.1.6 of the 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184 and now aligned with Section 66 of the RA No. 12009, the New Government Procurement Act. This is to promote transparency and accountability in the procurement process, in keeping with the updated legal framework.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the BAC, strictly ensure that all contractors comply with the prescribed minimum percentage requirements for surety bonds as provided under Section 6.2 of Annex “E” and Section 39.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to verify full compliance with bonding requirements and to protect the interests of the Municipality.  For future reference, these requirements are reiterated in Sections 68.4 and 71.2.8 of RA No. 12009, the New Government Procurement Act, which now consolidates and carries forward the provisions previously found in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor oblige the BAC to monitor the submission of the CSHP duly approved by the DOLE from the winning bidders before the signing of contract to ensure the protection and welfare of workers and the general public within and around the immediate vicinity of the construction worksite. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that payments of infrastructure projects are supported with valid Certificate of CSHP approved by the DOLE, otherwise, deduct the corresponding cost of implementing the same from the contractor’s claim to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Government require the Municipal Engineer to check the contractor’s billing by conducting inspection and verification of actual accomplishments documented through the preparation of a separate Inspection Report before certifying the amount to be paid to the contractor as progress payment. Moreover, the Inspection Report along with the other required documents should be attached to the disbursement vouchers to support the validity and propriety of progress payments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:

intensify the collection of real property taxes; and
issue notice of delinquency to all delinquent taxpayers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System Technical Working Group to:

prepare the GAD Plan and Budget to identify priority gender issues to be addressed by the LGU; and
ensure that that the gender issues/GAD mandates are in line with the Updated Gender Equality and Women’s Empowerment Plan 2019-2025 issued by the PCW which serves as reference and guide of LGUs in mainstreaming.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Government fully implement its SWMP and strictly adhere with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9003 to safeguard public health from substantial present and potentially hazardous wastes and attain a safe, clean and green environment.  We further recommend that Management comply with the documentary requirements of the DENR regarding the conversion of the proposed MRF site from forest land into special land use to enable the municipality to construct MRF and sanitary landfill therein.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a. Concerned contractors, through the Office of the Municipal Engineer, to prepare and submit SWA before the end of the year which shall serve as the basis of the Municipal Engineer in the conduct of inspection and validation of on-going and completed projects, and for proper determination of the percentage of completion to be reported in the QRGPPAs;

b. Municipal Accountant to recognize the unbilled accomplishments based on the verified SWA by the Municipal Engineer for the obligations of actual accomplishments of projects as at year-end; and

c. Municipal Accountant to cause the preparation of necessary journal entry/entries to correct the above-computed understatement of the CIP-Infrastructure Assets and Accounts Payable in the amount of ₱29,414,017.00, to present fairly the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason:
Review of QRGPPAs and verification of records disclosed that SWAs are already prepared and submitted by the concerned contractors and that there is no material deviation between the recognized CIP balances in the books an as per verified accomplishments reported in the said report

Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Government discontinue the practice of allowing payroll deductions on monthly loan amortizations of government/private institutions from the personnel’s salaries and other benefits that results in the net take home pay falling below the threshold provided in GAA of 2023. The withholding agent should strictly follow the order of preference in satisfying employees’ obligations as provided in the GAA to ensure that each employee receives net take home pay of not less than ₱5,000.00. Moreover, the personnel whose loan amortizations were not accommodated shall directly remit their payments to the collecting agent/office of the creditors.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
Concerned contractors, through the Office of the Municipal Engineer, to prepare and submit SWA before the end of the year which shall serve as the basis of the Municipal Engineer in the conduct of inspection and validation of on-going and completed projects, and for proper determination of the percentage of completion to be reported in the QRGPPAs;
Municipal Accountant to recognize the unbilled accomplishments based on the verified SWA by the Municipal Engineer for the obligations of actual accomplishments of projects as at year-end; and
Municipal Accountant to cause the preparation of necessary journal entry/entries to correct the above-computed understatement of the CIP-Infrastructure Assets and Accounts Payable in the amount of ₱29,414,017.00, to present fairly the affected accounts in the financial statements.

we strongly recommended that the Municipal Government intensify its efforts in preparing or retrieving available records to facilitate the formulation of appropriate strategies to recover the amount transferred to the above-mentioned NGOs/Pos; and
if said receivables had been determined to be highly improbable to collect, requests for write off which will serve as basis in derecognizing said items from the books of accounts thus, achieving fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to locate the details of the   accumulated savings from the PCF of the DILG and the balances of fund transfers from DA in the total amount of ₱437,606.01.  If diligent efforts to locate such records prove futile, prepare a written inquiry with the SAs if they have in their records receivables from the municipality of such amount as basis to return the excess amount, otherwise, prepare a journal entry voucher to transfer such amount to the General Fund and treat the same as income; and
the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to return the unused balance of fund transfers to the SA in the amount of ₱331,697.59 in compliance with Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 in order for the latter to utilize the same for other PPAs of other government agencies needing financial assistance.

We recommended that the Municipal Government discontinue the practice of allowing payroll deductions on monthly loan amortizations of government/private institutions from the personnel’s salaries and other benefits that results in the net take home pay falling below the threshold provided in GAA of 2023. The withholding agent should strictly follow the order of preference in satisfying employees’ obligations as provided in the GAA to ensure that each employee receives net take home pay of not less than ₱P5,000.00. Moreover, the personnel whose loan amortizations were not accommodated shall directly remit their payments to the collecting agent/office of the creditors.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Engineer to instruct all contractors to see to it that all laborers are provided with complete and appropriate PPEs and cause for the installation of warning devices at the project site whenever necessary in order not to compromise the health and safety of the workers and the public, and henceforth indicate the actual percentage of completion of the pay items in the SWA;
contractors to refund immediately the amount of ₱468,051.18 pertaining to the pay item for CSHP; and
Municipal Accountant to scrutinize the supporting documents of disbursement vouchers and cause for payment only on what is due to the contractors in order not to unduly benefit the latter at the expense of the municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Government fully implement its SWMP and strictly adhere with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9003 to safeguard public health from substantial present and potentially hazardous wastes and attain a safe, clean and green environment.  We further recommend that Management comply with the documentary requirements of the DENR regarding the conversion of the proposed MRF site from forest land into special land use to enable the municipality to construct MRF and sanitary landfill therein.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Health Officer to see to it that procurement of drugs and medicines are supported with complete documents as required in Section 9.1.3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, to ascertain the validity and propriety of the transactions and ensure the genuineness, effectivity and standard quality of the drugs and medicines purchased for the protection of the end users.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the required certified statements within the prescribed period to the LSB to ensure that the correct estimates of income for the ensuring year shall be used in the preparation of the SEF Budget and for the appropriation of proposed PPAs.

We recommended that Management, henceforth, ensure that all revenue and disbursement documents be submitted to the COA not later than the 10th day of the ensuing month to enable timely post audit of these transactions and immediately communicate deficiencies, if any, to management for appropriate corrective action.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit to the Auditor at the beginning of the year, a list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year for timely validation and monitoring of PPAs so that deficiencies, if any, could be communicated to management on time for appropriate action.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant, submit to the Audit Team all cancelled checks for inspection and proper monitoring.  Likewise, attach all cancelled checks to the RCI and report chronologically with the other checks issued and indicate the word “cancelled” on the report.

We recommended that Management:
Require the BAC and Municipal Engineer to review all contracts pertaining to the aforementioned infrastructure projects to determine the target date of completion, monitor if the contract time has expired and Management implement calibrated actions provided in GPPB Circular No. 03-2019 prior to termination in part or in whole, of the work that has not been completed and accepted under a contract;
Impose administrative sanctions, if warranted, in accordance with Section 69 of the IRR of RA No.  9184, to protect the interest of the government and to prevent similar incurrence of extended delays in the implementation of the projects; and
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to conduct necessary field surveys and investigations in the detailed engineering and carefully study/evaluate the site condition in the preparation of accurate design plans, technical specifications, quantity and cost estimates and program of work for infrastructure projects to minimize any work suspension that may cause delay in the completion of the projects thus, the interest of the government would not be compromised and the benefits would be delivered timely to its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to strictly monitor the implementation of infrastructure projects through validation of the contractor’s performance and by implementing calibrated measures if delays are incurred so as not to defer the immediate benefits that the constituents could derive therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipal Government, through the Municipal Engineering Office:
strictly require the contractor to prepare and submit the SWA and the Municipal Engineer to check the contractor’s billing by conducting inspection and verification of actual accomplishments documented through the preparation of a separate Inspection Report before certifying the amount to be paid to the contractor as progress payment.
require the Municipal Engineer that the Inspection Report along with the other required documents be attached to the disbursement vouchers to support the validity and propriety of progress payments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), require the winning bidder to post the performance security with validity period up to one year after completion date or until the issuance of Certificate of Final Acceptance to have assurance that the contractor will perform its obligation and to protect the government from loss due to defects that may occur.  In addition, require the BAC through the BAC TWG to ensure that performance securities are dated, with validity period and callable on demand.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC through its Secretariat to post the NOA in the PhilGEPS within 3 calendar days from the date of its issuance as required in Section 37.1.6 of the 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184 in order to promote transparency in the procurement process.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Chairman to instruct the BAC Secretariat to upload in the PhilGEPS website the complete set of bidding documents to invite more prospective bidders to participate in the bidding process by downloading the same free of charge and avoid the hassle of acquiring such documents from the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC through the TWG to conduct the post-qualification process in not more than 12 calendar days from the determination of LCB to avoid delays in the procurement process and in the implementation of the government projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE), through the BAC, issue NTP within seven calendar days from the date of approval of the contract as required in Section 37.4 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in order not to cause delays in the implementation of the projects and to ensure that the expected benefits to be derived therefrom are enjoyed by the target beneficiaries.

We recommended that the LCE require the GAD Focal Point System submit GPB and the corresponding AR to the Audit Team on time as required under Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 14, 2014.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Chairperson of the GFPS to fully implement the GAD PPAs to address gender issues/problems of the constituents in the municipal level and to help the national government attain its goal of addressing gender issues in the county.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
instruct the BAC to immediately start the procurement process of all the programs/projects funded out of the RA No. 7171 funds which were not yet subjected to public bidding, and
require all concerned Department Heads to facilitate the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to give the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds the early enjoyment of benefits they rightfully deserve. Thus, immediate implementation would enable the municipal government to avail of lesser project/program costs considering the escalating prices of all commodities, hence more benefits could be derived from these funds.

We recommended that Management to ensure that the 20% DF be optimally utilized to help achieve the desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the LGU as expressly prescribed under the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-01 dated November 4, 2020.

We recommended that Management adhere to Sections 9.4 and 10.4 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, regarding the settlement of suspensions within 90 calendar days from receipt of the NS, and disallowances shall be settled within six months from the receipt of the ND by the liable persons.
Furthermore, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require:
the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all disbursements are supported with valid and complete documents to avoid suspension in audit; and
the Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit immediately the lacking documents of infrastructure projects as required in the NS.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that henceforth, require the:

Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Accounting Office a schedule of unreleased checks at the end of every quarter, and

1.7.2	Municipal Accountant to restore the cash equivalent of all unreleased checks by debiting Cash in Bank – LCCA and crediting the appropriate payable/liability accounts supported with a working paper at the end of the quarter and by preparing a JEV at the end of the year based on the submitted schedule from the Municipal Treasurer.  In addition, in the ensuing year, prepare a JEV to reverse the entries made covering the restoration of cash balance in the previous year.

Thus, the balances of the affected accounts are fairly presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the BAC Chairman  require the BAC Secretariat to post the Notice of Award in the Philgeps within three (3) calendar days from its issuance.

We recommended that management require future contractors to submit duly accomplished performance security with a validity period that includes the one year defects liability period pursuant to Section 39 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. The validity period shall commence from the effectivity of the contract until one year from date of completion of the project or until the issuance of the Certificate of Acceptance.  Moreover, the Municipal Accountant being the internal auditor of the municipality should see to it that all supporting documents of disbursement vouchers are properly accomplished to ensure validity and reliability of the transactions.

We recommended that management require all contractors to post warranty security after the defects liability period of one year from project completion including the periods indicated in Sections 62.2.3.2  of the RIIR.  Such security must be in accordance with the schedules prescribed in Sections 62.2.3.3 of the same RIIR.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer intensify the collection of real property taxes,  conduct massive tax campaign in all barangays and offer tax amnesty in order to motivate the delinquent taxpayers to avail such offer.  Furthermore, seek the cooperation of barangay officials since they have also shares on the collection of real property taxes.

We recommended that the municipal government revisit the Memoranda of Agreement with the NGOs/POs together with individuals and exert extra effort to demand the liquidation of the funds/collection of the receivables.  Moreover, conduct an investigation on the status of these NGOs/POs and discuss with the appropriate committee in the Sangguniang Bayan any remedial measures that will be undertaken to the advantage of the municipality.    If such efforts exerted are futile, the Municipal Mayor may file the request for authority to write off said accounts to the COA Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor.  The request shall be supported by complete documents enumerated in Section 8.3 of COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineers Office closely monitor the implementation of infrastructure projects so that the plans and specifications and program of works/detailed estimates are strictly complied with by the contractors in order to deliver to the tobacco farmers-constituents the best quality projects they rightfully deserve and to avoid disallowance in audit.  If close monitoring of the implementation of the project could not be undertaken by the Municipal Engineers Office due to the various projects being implemented by the Municipality,  it is recommended that at least one member of the Barangay Councils  that is/are beneficiary/beneficiaries of the project monitor on a daily basis the implementation of infrastructure projects.  Consider the blacklisting of the contractor in accordance with GPPB rules.
We also recommended that management  address immediately the hanging carriage way which poses great danger to motorists and the riding public and may render the road totally unpassable  which would mean waste of money in the amount of more or less  P66,000,000.00  incurred for this road which came from the sweat of the tobacco farmers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to give the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds the early enjoyment of benefits they rightfully deserve.

We recommended that the Municipality stop the practice of procuring rolls of plastic black hose for potable water system because these are toxic to the human body.  Such rolls of plastic black hose should be replaced with GI pipes or plastic pipes suitable for drinking.  The Municipality through the Municipal Health Officer should seek the assistance of the national government through the latters SALINTUBIG Program in order to deliver potable water to the constituents.  The Municipality should take care of the health of tobacco farmers who are generating huge amount of money to the economy and to the municipality in the form of tobacco excise tax.

We recommended that management make constant follow up with the supplier regarding the repair of the 10-in-one heavy equipment in order to attain the purposes for which it was acquired hence maximizing its usage.  Moreover,  we recommended that management consider the hiring of a permanent employee as operator of the heavy equipment who shall be fully trained by the supplier.

We recommended that the municipal officials and employees concerned  implement fully all the programs/ projects / activities embodied in the 20% Development Fund in order to deliver on time the benefits the constituents could derive therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the prompt implementation of all funds received from other national government agencies in order to deliver on time the benefits due to the intended beneficiaries and to avoid returning the unutilized funds to the donor agencies.

We recommended that the municipality fully implement its Solid Waste Management Plan and the pertinent provisions of Republic Act. No. 9003 in order to safeguard the public health  from substantial present and potential hazardous wastes and attain a safe, clean and green environment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System to fully implement programs/projects funded under the GAD Plan in order to fully address gender issues/problems of the constituents in the municipal level and to help the national government attain its goal of addressing gender issues in the country.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant support the Public Infrastructures with registries for the years under the NGAS.   Also, other concerned offices should coordinate closely in order to assist the Municipal Accountant to comply with COA Circulars 2016-004 and 2015-008 with regard to accounting and reporting on the Local Roads Asset Management System in consistent with the adoption of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) in the LGU.  Also, the Local Chief Executive should enjoin the department heads’ compliance with the requirements of the circulars.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the municipality to submit the documentary requirements for the payment of honoraria to chair and members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Technical Working Group in order to establish the validity of disbursements in accordance with Section 4.6 of PD 1445 and provisions of DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A as amended by DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3 dated November 29, 2007.
observations.managementAction: Honoraria of BAC members were already supported with the necessary documents.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the GSO-designate to control the use of motor vehicles to prevent expeditious wear and tear that would result to excessive repair and maintenance. Trip tickets should be issued by the GSO-designate and approved by the Head of Agency to establish strong internal control.
observations.managementAction: Driver’s trip tickets were including these motor vehicles. GSO-designate assigned the Mayor to control the use of motor vehicles.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the municipality to submit the necessary supporting documents of those disbursement vouchers paid under the LDRRMF to establish the validity of the transactions. Also, maintain stock cards for the supplies inventories and small items and support the issuance of these stocks with Requisition Issue Slip (RIS). Moreover, refrain from charging capital outlay requirements of the LDRRM Office in order to avoid utilization of the fund not directly related to the implementation of programs on disaster risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRM Office uses logbook for the inventory and releases of goods and supplies.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the lapsing schedule of depreciation to support the provision of depreciation for the fair presentation of the expense and contra-asset accounts in the financial statements in accordance with the provision of Section 16 of PPSAS No. 16.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Section revised the depreciation schedule in accordance with PPSAS.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipality to employ adequate control on the consumption of gas and oil. Payment of gasoline expenses should be paid directly to suppliers so that appropriate taxes will be withheld and remitted. Also, credit line to gasoline station nearest to the Municipality may be engaged in order to refrain from reimbursements by the traveling employees. While those officials and employees receiving the full amount of RATA should refrain from reimbursing gasoline expenses because these are considered illegal expenditures. Moreover, record only expenses pertaining to gasoline consumption to Fuel, Oil and Lubricants Expenses
observations.managementAction: For the year 2016, Head of Offices and Elected Officials were not allowed to claim gasoline expenses. Some employees still reimbursed gasoline expenses.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to ascertain the completeness of his recording in the subsidiary ledger for all taxes withheld in order that the same are remitted within due date as required in the Revenue Regulation No 2-98 dated April 17, 1998.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Government were able to remit all taxes withheld to the BIR.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipality through the leadership of the Local Chief Executive to monitor the full implementation of the programs/projects appropriated under the 20% Development Fund in order to guarantee its optimal utilization of the fund in compliance with Section 5 of the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: For the year 2016, 70% of the fund was utilized. Verified in the management.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No.11, page 42 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the agency GAD Focal Point person to coordinate the preparation of the agency GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) with concerned offices or departments in order to appropriate at least 5% of the total budget appropriations of the agency. Moreover, implement all programs and GAD activities identified in the GBP in order to fully address the gender issues that will protect and fulfil the rights of women at the socio-cultural, economic and political spheres.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to appropriate 5% of the total budget appropriation for GAD and at least 70% was utilized.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 12, page 44 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to Municipal Government to discontinue their practice of settling the suspensions beyond the ninety (90) calendar days from receipt. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should be guided with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in the preparation of disbursement vouchers with the supporting documents to avoid suspensions and disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Settlements of suspensions are still on going. Some suspensions were settled during the year.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 13, page 45 of the report.
